teams.name: Argentina
teams.style: Flexible 4-4-2/4-3-3, controlled possession with vertical bursts, world-class game management and a ferocious counter-press around Messi's free role
keyPlayers: Lionel Messi (Inter Miami, captain — still the fulcrum at 38)
Julián Álvarez (Atlético Madrid, ST)
Alexis Mac Allister (Liverpool, CM)
Emiliano Martínez (Aston Villa, GK)
teams.form: W 3-0 Iceland, W 2-0 Honduras (June friendlies); top of CONMEBOL qualifying; 17 of the Qatar 2022 winners retained.
teams.outlook: Overwhelming favourites: Opta gives 72% to top the group. Only heat, Messi's hamstring and complacency stand between them and the last 32.

teams.name: Austria
teams.style: Aggressive gegenpressing 4-2-2-2 — the lowest PPDA of all UEFA qualifiers, relentless transitions and structured set pieces
keyPlayers: David Alaba (Real Madrid, captain, CB — finally at a World Cup at 33)
Marcel Sabitzer (Dortmund, CM — led qualifying for chances created)
Christoph Baumgartner (RB Leipzig, AM — career-best 13 Bundesliga goals)
Marko Arnautović (Red Star Belgrade, ST — 8 qualifying goals)
teams.form: W 1-0 Tunisia (June 1); topped qualifying group, first World Cup since 1998. Drilled to a Rangnick rhythm.
teams.outlook: Opta's pick for second (67.4% to advance). The press travels; the question is whether ageing legs survive Texas heat.

teams.name: Algeria
teams.style: Patient 4-3-3 possession with devastating left-sided overloads; Petković's calm structure has tightened a once-chaotic defence
keyPlayers: Riyad Mahrez (Al-Ahli, captain, RW — 38 international goals)
Mohamed Amoura (Wolfsburg, FW — involved in 58% of Algeria's qualifying goals)
Rayan Aït-Nouri (Manchester City, LB)
Ibrahim Maza (Bayer Leverkusen, AM)
teams.form: Stunned the Netherlands 1-0 in Rotterdam (June 3); 8 qualifying wins, their best-ever campaign.
teams.outlook: Live dark horse at 57.1% (Opta) to advance. Amoura's pace against Austria's high line on the final matchday could decide second place.

teams.name: Jordan
teams.style: Compact 5-4-1 low-to-mid block, rapid counters through the right channel toward Al-Tamari; set-piece dependent
keyPlayers: Musa Al-Tamari (Rennes, W, captain — the 'Jordanian Messi')
Yazan Al-Naimat (FW — Asian Cup 2023 final hero)
Yazan Al-Arab (CB)
teams.form: L 1-4 Switzerland, L 0-2 Colombia in June; Arab Cup 2025 runners-up; 32 qualifying goals — their best ever.
teams.outlook: Brave debutants (Opta 40.9%) but warm-up defeats exposed the gulf. The Algeria game in Santa Clara is their realistic point-harvest.

groups.groupReading: The cards show a crown that does not slip: the champions of Lusail glide through, untroubled by desert winds or alpine presses. Behind them, the Austrian machine and the Algerian desert fox circle one another until the final night in Kansas City, where a single goal divides destiny.

matches.reading: The spirits whisper of 2010's ghost — but this Argentina is no haunted side. Algeria's triumph in Rotterdam glows bright, yet the crystal shows Álvarez's press suffocating their build-up and Messi conjuring once under the Arrowhead lights. The fox runs bravely, but the lion eats first.
matches.keyFactor: Argentina's counter-press vs Algeria's short build-up

matches.reading: I see a red machine of pistons and pressing traps meeting a debutant still blinking in the World Cup glare. Jordan's warm-up wounds — four conceded to Switzerland, two to Colombia — foretell a back line overwhelmed by Baumgartner's late runs. Al-Tamari may flash like a comet, but comets do not stop machines.
matches.keyFactor: Rangnick's press against a back line that shipped 6 in two friendlies

matches.reading: A noon sun over Texas, though the great roof shields the worst of it. Austria's press will steal one moment of chaos — the orb shows Sabitzer striking — but pressing Messi's Argentina for ninety minutes is to chase smoke. The champions' extra class in both boxes tilts the scales late.
matches.keyFactor: Whether Austria can sustain an elite press against elite ball retention

matches.reading: Two Arab nations under a California dusk, and the cards favour the elder brother. Amoura's lightning will find the space behind Jordan's wing-backs that Switzerland found before him. Yet the debutants' pride is real — Al-Tamari etches his name on the scoresheet before the night belongs to Algeria.
matches.keyFactor: Amoura's pace in behind Jordan's adventurous wing-backs

matches.reading: The decisive rune: whoever wins likely takes second, and the orb clouds with tension. Austria's press meets Mahrez's guile in a duel of nerve, and I see weary Austrian legs after two high-octane outings conceding late possession. A point each — and both pass through on the strength of earlier deeds, Austria above by the Jordan margin.
matches.keyFactor: Austrian pressing fatigue in a third match in eleven days

matches.reading: By the third night the champions will already hold the key to the next door, and Scaloni will rest some princes — yet even Argentina's second court outranks Jordan's first. The debutants defend with hearts of bronze, but bronze bends. Two goals, no reply, and Messi waves to Dallas from the bench's golden throne.
matches.keyFactor: Argentina's squad depth even with rotation

teams.name: Portugal
teams.style: 3-4-3/4-3-3 hybrid, technical midfield dominance through the PSG axis, patient possession with Ronaldo as penalty-box finisher
keyPlayers: Cristiano Ronaldo (Al-Nassr, captain, ST — 41, record caps, sixth World Cup)
Vitinha (PSG, CM — Europe's premier tempo-controller)
Bernardo Silva (Manchester City, AM)
Nuno Mendes (PSG, LB)
teams.form: W 2-1 Nigeria (June 10), W 2-1 Chile (June 6); reigning Nations League champions, qualified top of their group.
teams.outlook: Opta: 59% to win the group, 94.9% to advance. The deepest squad in Group K; the only doubt is whether Ronaldo-centricity blunts them against elite pressing later — and two opening games in Houston heat.

teams.name: Colombia
teams.style: 4-2-3-1 with explosive wide play, Díaz cutting in from the left, James operating between lines; press-resistant and street-smart
keyPlayers: Luis Díaz (Bayern Munich, LW — one of the form wingers of 2025-26)
James Rodríguez (Minnesota United, AM — the eternal conductor)
Jhon Arias (Wolverhampton, W)
Dávinson Sánchez (Galatasaray, CB)
teams.form: W 2-0 Jordan (June 8), W 3-1 Costa Rica (June 2); 28 qualifying goals, second only to Argentina; Copa América 2024 finalists.
teams.outlook: 84.9% to advance (Opta) and a fashionable pick to win the group. Comfortable at Azteca altitude; the Miami finale against Portugal likely decides top spot.

teams.name: Uzbekistan
teams.style: Disciplined 4-1-4-1 Italian-school block, compact central spine, counters funnelled to Shomurodov and Fayzullaev
keyPlayers: Abdukodir Khusanov (Manchester City, CB — short of minutes and sharpness)
Eldor Shomurodov (captain, ST — all-time top scorer)
Abbosbek Fayzullaev (AM — the creative jewel)
teams.form: L 1-2 Netherlands (June 8), L 0-2 Canada (June 2); superb qualifying record to reach a first-ever World Cup, but June friendlies exposed the level gap.
teams.outlook: Opta: 22.1% to advance. Cannavaro's organisation gives them a puncher's chance, but Khusanov's rust and two warm-up defeats temper the debut dream.

teams.name: DR Congo
teams.style: Physical, well-drilled 4-3-3 mid-block; athletic transitions through Wissa and Bakambu; resilient knockout temperament forged in playoffs
keyPlayers: Yoane Wissa (Newcastle United, ST)
Chancel Mbemba (Lille, captain, CB — 107 caps)
Cédric Bakambu (FW)
Axel Tuanzebe (DF — playoff hero vs Jamaica)
teams.form: D 0-0 Denmark (June 3), L 1-2 Chile (June 9); won the intercontinental playoff to reach a first finals since Zaire 1974.
teams.outlook: Opta: 22.1% to advance. Defensively credible — the Denmark draw proved it — but goals are scarce, and Houston's noon heat greets them first.

groups.groupReading: Two crowned heads — one Iberian, one Andean — march in step toward the gilded gates, their final waltz in Miami merely deciding who leads the procession. Beneath them, the Leopard and the White Wolf claw for scraps of history, and the cards favour the Leopard's teeth over the Wolf's weary legs.

matches.reading: Fifty-two years the Leopards have waited, and fate hands them Vitinha's metronome under a Houston roof. The orb shows Congo's block holding an hour — as it held Denmark — before Mendes's overlaps stretch the seams and Ronaldo, in his sixth chapter, strikes again. Brave claws, but the empire advances.
matches.keyFactor: Portugal's full-back overloads breaking a deep block after the hour

matches.reading: At 2,200 metres the air thins, and it is the children of the Andes who breathe easiest. The crystal shows Khusanov, starved of minutes at City, chasing Díaz's shadow into the Azteca night. Cannavaro's wall is honest stone, but Arias and Díaz arrive in flame — the debutants' dream dims at altitude.
matches.keyFactor: Azteca altitude favouring Andean lungs over a rusty Khusanov

matches.reading: The White Wolves will sit deep and snap at heels, as Cannavaro taught them, but the cards show Bernardo Silva threading needles no block can close forever. Shomurodov hunts alone on the counter while Portugal's PSG spine strangles the middle. Two goals, scant reply — the Wolves' fate passes to the final night.
matches.keyFactor: Portugal's midfield technicians vs a low block with no outlet

matches.reading: The Leopards travel from Houston's furnace to Guadalajara's heights, and tired sinews tell tales. Wissa will land one blow — Newcastle steel does not rust — yet the orb shows James, in his twilight, sliding the pass that frees Díaz to settle it. Colombia's rhythm, honed by altitude and momentum, prevails by a whisker.
matches.keyFactor: Colombia's altitude comfort and Díaz's left-channel dominance

matches.reading: Miami — Messi's city, yet tonight a duel of Ronaldo and Díaz, likely with both passages already booked. The cards show caution dressed as spectacle: Martínez protecting his princes, Lorenzo trusting his unbeaten spell. A goal apiece in the humid night, and Portugal's superior earlier arithmetic keeps the crown.
matches.keyFactor: Both sides likely qualified — rotation blunts the spectacle

matches.reading: Under Atlanta's closed sky, two debut dreams collide for third place and a possible golden ticket onward. The Wolves have not scored freely all June; the Leopards carry Tuanzebe's playoff sorcery and Wissa's Premier League edge. I see one goal — Congolese, late, physical — and history made for Kinshasa.
matches.keyFactor: DR Congo's physicality vs Uzbekistan's blunt June attack

teams.name: England
teams.style: Pragmatic 4-2-3-1, German-engineered defensive structure, fast wide transitions through Saka and Gordon, Kane dropping to link
keyPlayers: Harry Kane (Bayern Munich, captain, ST — 36 Bundesliga goals in 31 games)
Jude Bellingham (Real Madrid, AM)
Bukayo Saka (Arsenal, RW)
Declan Rice (Arsenal, DM)
teams.form: W 3-0 Costa Rica (June 10), W 1-0 New Zealand (June 6); perfect qualifying — 8 wins, 22 scored, 8 clean sheets. Foden and Palmer left home.
teams.outlook: Opta: 67.5% to win the group, 11.2% to win the tournament (3rd favourite). Ruthless under Tuchel; the only flicker of doubt is Bellingham's rhythm and knockout scar tissue.

teams.name: Croatia
teams.style: 4-3-3 midfield-control football, Modrić advanced as a No.10 to spare his legs; slow build, set-piece danger, peerless tournament streetcraft
keyPlayers: Luka Modrić (AC Milan, captain, AM — 40, record sixth World Cup)
Joško Gvardiol (Manchester City, CB — just back from a fractured tibia)
Mateo Kovačić (Manchester City, CM — missed most of season with Achilles trouble)
Ivan Perišić (W — the eternal tournament performer)
teams.form: W 2-1 Slovenia (June 7), L 0-2 Belgium (June 2); unbeaten qualifying campaign. Fitness of Gvardiol and Kovačić is the great unknown.
teams.outlook: Opta: 76.9% to advance. Finalists 2018, third 2022 — but the golden midfield is 40, 36 and patched-up. Likely second, dangerous to anyone in a one-off knockout.

teams.name: Ghana
teams.manager: Carlos Queiroz (appointed after Otto Addo's March sacking)
teams.style: Emergency-rebuilt 4-4-2 low block, defensive caution with explosive breaks via Semenyo and Williams
keyPlayers: Antoine Semenyo (Manchester City, FW — 17 PL goals this season)
Jordan Ayew (Leicester City, captain, FW — 14 goal involvements in qualifying)
Thomas Partey (Villarreal, DM)
Iñaki Williams (Athletic Club, FW)
teams.form: D 1-1 Wales (June 2); 1 win in last 5. Mohammed Kudus OUT injured — he scored the goal that qualified them. No clean sheet in their last 10 World Cup games.
teams.outlook: Opta: 49.5% to advance, but chaos reigns: new coach, no Kudus, poor form. Semenyo's brilliance is the lottery ticket.

teams.name: Panama
teams.style: Energetic 4-4-2 pressing unit — led CONCACAF qualifying in high turnovers and pressed sequences; wing-back thrust through Murillo
keyPlayers: Adalberto Carrasquilla (Pumas UNAM, AM — the creative engine)
Amir Murillo (RB — 9 goals 12 assists in 90 caps)
José Fajardo (ST — 17 international goals)
José Córdoba (Norwich City, CB)
teams.form: D 1-1 Bosnia (June 6), L 2-6 Brazil (May 31), W 4-2 Dominican Republic; qualified unbeaten through both CONCACAF rounds.
teams.outlook: Opta: 40% to advance — group outsiders, but organised, unbeaten in qualifying, and far better than the 2018 vintage. The Ghana opener is everything.

groups.groupReading: The Three Lions march behind a German drum, their qualifying ledger spotless — eight gates, eight clean sheets — and the cards see no force in this quartet to stain it. Behind them the old Croatian sorcerer conjures one last passage, while the Black Stars and the Canal-men wrestle in the dust for a third-place lifeline.

matches.reading: The marquee duel of the opening week, and the orb remembers Moscow 2018 — but the wheel has turned. Modrić at 40 still breaks lines like a whisper breaks silence, yet Tuchel's England are iron where Southgate's were glass, and Kane arrives gorged on 36 Bayern goals. Croatia score from a set piece; England score twice from the run of play.
matches.keyFactor: England's press targeting Croatia's 40-year-old playmaker and half-fit spine

matches.reading: Two troubled caravans meet under Toronto's cool sky — one shorn of Kudus and led by a coach of mere weeks, the other still bleeding from six Brazilian wounds. The cards show Semenyo's lightning answered by Panama's swarming press and a Murillo surge. Honours even, and both will rue it on the final day.
matches.keyFactor: Ghana's managerial upheaval vs Panama's organised press — neither can dominate

matches.reading: The crystal is unusually clear: a side with eight straight qualifying clean sheets against one that has not kept a single one in ten World Cup outings. Semenyo will frighten his City clubmates once or twice on the break, but Saka, Kane and the Bellingham of big occasions feast on Queiroz's hastily poured concrete. Three for the Lions.
matches.keyFactor: Ghana's chronic defensive leaks against England's clinical depth

matches.reading: Panama's brave press will hunt Modrić in packs, as the numbers say they must — but the old sorcerer has escaped finer cages for two decades. The orb shows Kovačić and Modrić passing through the storm until Panamanian legs empty, then Perišić and a substitute strike in the closing half-hour. Experience devours enthusiasm.
matches.keyFactor: Croatia's press-resistant midfield outlasting Panama's high-energy game

matches.reading: At the great arena where the final itself will be decided, England rehearse. Tuchel will rotate, yet the bench hungers for July shirts — and Panama, needing a miracle, must open up and perish by it. Two goals, another clean sheet, and the Lions take a perfect nine into the knockout labyrinth.
matches.keyFactor: England's bench strength against a Panama side forced to chase

matches.reading: Philadelphia, city of underdogs — but the cards do not read scripts. Ghana must win and will finally unchain Semenyo and Williams, landing one true blow; yet Croatia, needing only composure, have made a religion of decisive group-stage nights. Modrić's wand and a Gvardiol set-piece header send the chequered shirts through and the Black Stars home, third place their slender thread.
matches.keyFactor: Croatia's tournament know-how in must-not-lose finales
